title: Pilot Stirling PDF locally
goal: Pilot Stirling PDF as a local PDF workbench after service-boundary and backup prerequisites are clear.

acceptance.assertion: Stirling PDF is bound only to localhost or Tailnet and has a documented rollback/stop path.
A dummy PDF split/merge/OCR or equivalent smoke proves the workflow without sensitive documents.
Output folders are separated from canonical document archives and reviewed before later import.

metadata.source_request: Evaluate every interesting utility tool and register the resulting plan in Bureau.
validation: Infra PR #107 GitHub CI validate passed.
scripts/utilities/heim-utilities-smoke.sh reported Backrest, Beszel, Stirling PDF, Paperless, Atuin, Difftastic, rga and LocalSend OK.
Restic util-base-v1 snapshot and single-file restore probe passed.
Paperless imported synthetic PDF document id 1 and indexed content containing UTIL-BASE-V1.
Stirling PDF converted the probe PDF to text and merged two copies into a 2-page PDF.
Docling 2.111.0 converted the probe PDF to Markdown and JSON with UTIL-BASE-V1 content.
metadata.outcome: Implemented in infra PR #107 and live-smoked on heim-pc; see runtime evidence for exact boundaries and remaining manual steps.

verified_acceptance.util-base-v1-t007-1: Stirling PDF runs on 127.0.0.1:8084 with SECURITY_ENABLELOGIN=false.
verified_acceptance.util-base-v1-t007-2: Synthetic PDF was converted to text and merged into a 2-page PDF via local API.
verified_acceptance.util-base-v1-t007-3: Probe outputs are under /home/alex/.local/state/heim-utilities/stirling-probe, separate from canonical archives.
